skill.name: pinchbench
skill.description: Run PinchBench benchmarks to evaluate OpenClaw agent performance across real-world tasks. Use when testing model capabilities, comparing models, submitting benchmark results to the leaderboard, or checking how well your OpenClaw setup handles calendar, email, research, coding, and multi-step workflows.
skill.summary: Benchmark OpenClaw agent performance with 23 real-world tasks and submit results to the PinchBench leaderboard.
skill.author: pinchbench

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 573 potential issues, but evaluation confirms most are false positives. Critical findings (recursive delete, pipe-to-shell) reference standard Docker installation patterns from trusted sources. High-severity 'weak crypto' findings detect MD5 used for checksums, not security. Markdown documentation backticks were misidentified as shell execution. True positives (subprocess, network, env access) are expected for benchmark functionality and properly scoped.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for External Commands
medium_findings.description: Python subprocess.run used to execute git commands and OpenClaw CLI. Arguments are hardcoded or validated, but subprocess execution always carries injection risk if inputs are not properly sanitized.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run with hardcoded git arguments for version detection - legitimate use but requires monitoring for future input handling changes

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Keys
low_findings.description: Script reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY from environment for model validation. This is standard practice but requires users to properly secure their API keys.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of API key access for legitimate OpenRouter model validation - documented security boundary

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External APIs
low_findings.description: HTTP requests made to openrouter.ai for model validation and pinchbench.com for result uploads. All endpoints are official and documented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls to documented official endpoints for core functionality - expected behavior for benchmark submission

content.user_title: Benchmark OpenClaw Agent Performance Across 23 Real-World Tasks
content.value_statement: Testing LLM models as AI agents requires consistent, measurable evaluation. PinchBench provides 23 diverse tasks spanning calendar management, email handling, research, coding, and multi-step workflows with automated grading and public leaderboard submission.
actual_capabilities: Execute 23 benchmark tasks covering productivity, research, coding, writing, and multi-step workflows
Validate OpenRouter model availability before running benchmarks
Automated grading with mean score calculation per task
Token efficiency tracking including input/output tokens and cost estimation
Submit results to PinchBench public leaderboard with API token authentication
Support custom task suites and individual task selection
limitations: Requires Python 3.10+ and uv package manager installed
Requires OpenClaw instance configured and accessible
Leaderboard submission requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable
Network access required for model validation and result uploads

use_cases.title: Model Comparison for Agent Selection
use_cases.description: Compare multiple LLM models to determine which performs best as an OpenClaw agent for your specific use cases.

use_cases.title: Continuous Performance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track agent performance over time as models are updated or configurations change.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps teams managing AI agent deployments

use_cases.title: Community Benchmark Contribution
use_cases.description: Submit your benchmark results to the public leaderboard to help the community understand model capabilities.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Benchmark Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run PinchBench with Claude Sonnet 4 model on all automated tasks. Show me the overall score and identify the top 3 and bottom 3 performing tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Specific Task Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Run PinchBench benchmark for task_01_calendar, task_02_stock, and task_09_files only. Compare results between anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 and anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet models.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused evaluation on specific capability areas

prompt_templates.title: Full Benchmark with Leaderboard Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute complete PinchBench suite with anthropic/claude-opus-4 model. Validate the model first, run all 23 tasks with 2 runs each for averaging, and submit results to the PinchBench leaderboard using my registered API token.

prompt_templates.title: Token Efficiency Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run PinchBench automated tasks and generate a token efficiency report. Show score per token and score per dollar for each task. Identify which tasks have the best and worst efficiency ratios.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cost optimization analysis for production deployment

output_examples.input: Run PinchBench with claude-sonnet-4 on automated tasks
output_examples.output: Benchmark completed: 15/23 tasks executed. Overall score: 0.78. Top performers: task_00_sanity (0.95), task_01_calendar (0.89), task_07_email (0.85). Tasks requiring review: task_16_email_triage (0.42), task_18_market_research (0.38). Total execution time: 47 minutes. Token efficiency: 2.3 score points per 1000 tokens.

output_examples.input: Compare claude-sonnet-4 vs claude-opus-4 on coding tasks
output_examples.output: Model comparison results: claude-sonnet-4 average: 0.72, claude-opus-4 average: 0.84. Opus-4 shows significant improvement on task_04_weather (+0.25) and task_10_workflow (+0.18). Sonnet-4 performs comparably on task_00_sanity. Cost analysis: Opus-4 costs 2.3x more per task but delivers 17% higher scores.

best_practices: Run benchmarks with at least 2-3 runs per task to account for variance in LLM outputs
Use --no-upload flag during development to avoid polluting leaderboard with test results
Register for API token before first submission and store OPENROUTER_API_KEY securely in environment
anti_patterns: Do not submit benchmark results from modified task definitions - results will be rejected
Avoid running full benchmark suite without --timeout-multiplier for slower models
Do not share API tokens or commit them to version control

faq.question: What is PinchBench and what does it measure?
faq.answer: PinchBench is a benchmark suite with 23 real-world tasks that evaluate how well LLM models perform as the brain of an OpenClaw agent. It measures task completion across categories like calendar management, email handling, research, coding, and multi-step workflows.

faq.question: How do I submit results to the leaderboard?
faq.answer: First register for an API token using 'uv run benchmark.py --register'. Then run your benchmark normally - results are automatically uploaded if you have a valid token. View results at pinchbench.com.

faq.question: Can I run benchmarks without an internet connection?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --no-upload flag to skip leaderboard submission. However, model validation requires internet access to verify model availability on OpenRouter.

faq.question: How long does a full benchmark run take?
faq.answer: A complete run of all 23 tasks typically takes 30-60 minutes depending on the model speed and timeout settings. Use --suite automated-only for faster runs, or --timeout-multiplier to adjust timeouts for slower models.

faq.question: What models are supported?
faq.answer: Any model available through OpenRouter is supported. Common choices include anthropic/claude-sonnet-4, anthropic/claude-opus-4, and various open-source models. Use the model validation feature to verify availability before running.

faq.question: How are tasks graded?
faq.answer: Each task has automated grading criteria defined in its markdown file. Grading produces a mean score between 0 and 1, where 1 indicates perfect task completion. Some tasks include multiple checks that are averaged into the final score.
